root_cause.primary: 74M cache read tokens are from THIS DevAgent session's conversation history being replayed on every message — the session is now ~18 hours old with hundreds of exchanges. This is expected Anthropic caching behaviour; the cache is being read (cheap) not written (expensive). The conversation length itself is the cost driver.
root_cause.secondary: Workspace files at root were growing — outreach drafts (10-17KB each), improvements files (13-15KB), result_v9.json (16KB) all auto-loaded as context on every agent call

standing_rules_added: Outreach draft files should be archived after 48 hours — they are for immediate use only
improvements_*.md files should be trimmed monthly to last 10 entries
result_v*.json files should never be left in the workspace root — always write to GitHub
note_on_74m_cache_reads: Cache reads at $0.30/million are 10x cheaper than writes. 74M cache reads = ~$22 vs the same tokens as writes would be ~$277. The caching is working correctly and saving money. The real cost is the session history length — starting a new DevAgent session tomorrow will reset this.
